aboutme.title: ABOUT ME
aboutme.paragraph_one: Hi! I'm Alexandra and I'm a Full-Stack Developer with a strong foundation in architecture and project management. My journey from designing physical spaces to creating digital products has strengthened my analytical thinking, creativity, and user-centered mindset. I enjoy building clean, maintainable web applications using modern technologies.
aboutme.paragraph_two: Curious and continuous learner, I'm passionate about technology that improves people's lives. I aim to contribute what I've learned both technically and personally to build meaningful, efficient, and sustainable digital products.
aboutme.paragraph_three_start: With these knowledge, skills and the
aboutme.paragraph_three_end: I handle I feel that I can contribute to the needs of society through development.

tools.title: TECHNOLOGIES AND TOOLS
tools.paragraph_one: I have completed several bootcamps and courses, where I learned to manage projects using agile methodologies and to apply SOLID principles, develop with TDD, and perform unit and feature testing. I also earned a UX Design certificate and hold credentials in Green Software, as sustainable development and green coding are areas I'm passionate about.
tools.paragraph_two: For UI design, I use tools such as Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, and Figma. In agile workflows, I rely on Jira and Trello. My development stack includes HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, React, Next.js, PHP, Laravel, Java, Spring Boot, Python, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Node.js, JWT and more. For testing, I use Jest, React Testing Library, Vitest, PHPUnit, and JUnit, and most of my projects are available on GitHub.
tools.paragraph_three: To assess accessibility, I use Axe DevTools and PageSpeed Insights, and to evaluate sustainability, I rely on Digital Beacon and the Website Carbon Calculator.

green.title: GREEN SOFTWARE
green.paragraph_one: For me, it's essential to live consciously on this planet and take care of it, embracing all its complexity and seeking harmony with everything that surrounds us. I know that achieving this balance within today's system is challenging, but not impossible. Every small step counts and can transform how we relate to our environment.
green.paragraph_two: Technology and the internet are highly polluting, and I want to challenge myself to prove that it's possible to develop good software with minimal environmental impact.
green.paragraph_three: I have analyzed this website using several tools that measure the carbon footprint of web pages. According to Digital Beacon, this portfolio performs amazingly well in terms of its environmental impact.
green.alt_digital_beacon: This website's Digital Beacon rating is incredible when it comes to its carbon footprint.
green.alt_page_speed: Page speed insights results for this website: 97% for performance, 100% accesibility, 100% recommended practices, 100% SEO.
green.alt_green_host: This website is hosted Green - checked by thegreenwebfoundation.org
green.alt_carbon_calculator: The website carbon calculator rate this page as cleaner than 97% of web pages tested.

1.subtitle: klik group landing page
1.description: This is a static website design only for desktop. It is not responsive. It serves as an introduction to our team and our state of mind. The goal is to learn how to use HTML5 and CSS3 without any JavaScript functions. We aimed for a simple and elegant design made in Figma based on a template.

2.subtitle: oline catalog for a hat store
2.description: This is a static web page, a catalogue that sells hats for both women and men. It is for a traditional and antique store in Barcelona. It is made with HTML5, CSS3 and JavaScript. The page is not finished and the responsive version is missing; it only works well on devices with a resolution of 375 x 812 pixels.

3.subtitle: random sacrifice selection app
3.description: The goal of the Wheel of Doom is to create a fair and random system for selecting sacrifices among coders, like a video game. It is intended to eliminate players. It is responsive and made with HTML5, CSS3 and at least four JavaScript functions, including audio and animation.

4.subtitle: appointment scheduling app
4.description: Handy App allows company workers to request an appointment with the CTO to solve technical problems within the development team. The CTO can see all the appointments requested by the coders, and the coders can modify their request information. User security features are not implemented yet. It was made with HTML5, CSS3, Sass, PHP, Figma, MySQL, and PHPMyAdmin-PDO.

5.subtitle: online events management app
5.description: This app was created to manage online events such as workshops, masterclasses, or webinars. Users can view event descriptions, sign up, and unsubscribe. They can also see the list of events they have registered for. The administrator can create, edit, and delete events, which are saved on a history page. It follows an MVC architecture and was built using PHP 7.4, Laravel 8.0, Auth, Blade, Bootstrap, and MySQL.

6.subtitle: all-purpose donation app
6.description: This is a donation app that works like an e-commerce platform but without a payment gateway. People can donate any kind of items and also reserve those they wish to receive. Users earn Klikcoins for the donations they make, which can be accumulated and exchanged for rewards. This app has two repositories: one for the front end and one for the back end. It is not deployed yet, and the front end consumes the back end through the API using tokens to validate users. It follows an MVC architecture and uses PHP 7.4, Laravel 8.0, MySQL, Bootstrap, and Vue.

7.subtitle: e-commerce for a comic store
7.description: Application created to meet the growing sales demand of a comics store located in Oviedo - Spain, which saw its sales increase significantly during the pandemic. The company needed to create a solid and scalable application, initially to cover its online store functionality and in the future to complement the other online services that Hangar Rebelde offers. MVC architecture and technologies used were PHP 7.4, Laravel 8.0, Auth, Blade, Bootstrap, Sass, Stripe (API), MySql, PHPUnit for TDD and unit tests.

8.subtitle: star wars spaceship search website
8.description: This is a website that lets you find a list of ships belonging to the Star Wars saga. It is developed with React and consumes the Star Wars API (https://swapi.dev/api/starships) using Axios requests from a service. It also includes pagination. There are two user roles: a regular user, who can access the home page, and a premium user, who can access the list of ships and detailed pages including films and pilots. Authorization currently uses localStorage.

9.subtitle: recipe search website
9.description: This is a website that lets you search for dessert recipes. It is developed with React and consumes the Edamam API (https://www.edamam.com/) using Axios requests from a service. It uses react-router-dom v6 to navigate between pages. There are two user roles: a regular user, who can search for recipes, and a premium user, who can access specific desserts and full recipe links. Authorization currently uses localStorage. The app is component-based and styled with styled-components. It's responsive.

10.subtitle: ux design for a mobile ticketing web
10.description: Movie theater app is a web that allows you to buy movie tickets for a specific cinema in the city of Barcelona and offers snack and drink packs with discounts. It's a complete user experience (UX) design including user research and design of wireframes, mockups and prototypes. The 'repository' button contains the case study that shows the entire research and design process. The project was carried out for the course of Google User Experience (UX) Design, Professional Certificate.

11.subtitle: ux design for a pet shelter web
11.description: Pet Adoption App is a responsive application that allows users to view, choose, adopt, or sponsor a pet from an animal shelter. It is a complete user experience (UX) design including user research, wireframes, mockups, and prototypes. It is designed with a graceful degradation philosophy. The 'repository' button contains the case study that shows the entire research and design process. The project was carried out for the Google User Experience (UX) Design Professional Certificate course.

12.subtitle: ux design for a city shelter web
12.description: City Shelter App is a responsive web application that allows you to search for a shelter for people who need it in the city of Barcelona, view information about the shelter, its capacity, occupation and vacant spaces, as well as being able to register a person and reserve their space. It's a total design of user experience (UX) including user research and design of wireframes, mockups and prototypes. It's designed with a progressive enhancement philosophy (mobile first). The 'repository' button contains the case study that shows the entire research and design process. The project was carried out for the course of Google User Experience (UX) Design, Professional Certificate.

13.subtitle: green improvement of Factoría F5 website
13.description: This project was carried out as the final work of the Green Digital Skills Certificate, the objective of this study is to measure Factoría F5 organization's landing page, generate an improvement plan and develop a replica from the original page (at an educational level) to compare the initial and final carbon footprint, after following some recommendations, without compromising its design and quality. The original page is developed in Wordpress and the replica in React, so it will also be possible to have a comparison between technologies, it will only be measured in desktop version. The full analysis can be found at the prototype link.

1.subtitle: my personal resume website
1.description: This is my resume made with HTML5, CSS3 and vanilla JavaScript. It has a flirtatious design where I show different information distributed in a lateral navigation bar. I added some CSS animations for a more dynamic experience. I made the UX/UI design in Figma for both desktop and mobile. It's responsive.

2.subtitle: online catalog for a macrame store
2.description: This is a product catalogue with a payment gateway (Stripe consumed through an API) and chat (WhatsApp consumed through an API), made with HTML5, CSS3 and vanilla JavaScript. I made the UX/UI design in Figma for both desktop and mobile, and the development was done as a team. It's responsive.
